Ideal Market Chain proposes an interior renovation of the existing grocery store located at 3033 South Carrollton Ave, previously occupied by Save-A-Lot. The scope of work includes a complete interior remodel designed to align with Ideal Market’s current design standards. Improvements consist of new interior finishes, updated interior paint, revised layout of sales area, upgraded and new equipment, and installation of new shelving and fixtures. No exterior alterations are proposed at this time. Exterior Signage will be under a separate permit.

THE PROJECT SCOPE IS RESTRICTED TO INTERIOR RENOVATIONS OF SELECT SPACES THROUGHOUT THE SUB-BASEMENT, BASEMENT, LEVEL 01, LEVEL 02 & LEVEL 03 AT THE ROYAL SONESTA LOCATED AT 300 BOURBON ST. IN NEW ORLEANS, LA., AND DOES NOT AFFECT THE EXISTING OCCUPANCY, EXISTING OCCUPANCY COUNTS OR EXISTING EGRESS ROUTES. SPACES INCLUDED IN THE RENOVATION ARE OUTLINED BELOW: SUB-BASEMENT: ROYAL/CONTI, BACCHUS, BOURBON, CORRIDOR, & STAIR 12, BASEMENT STAIR 12, FITNESS ROOM, WOMENS & MENS RR, MOTOR LOBBY LEVEL 1: MAIN BALLROOM, PREFUNCTION, EVANGELINE (A, B, C), VESTIBULE, REGAL ROOM, ARCADE, ESPLANADE, IBERVILLE, SOLEIL & SOULE, ENTRANCE LOBBY, HOTEL LOBBY, LE BOOZE, JAZZ PLAYHOUSE, COURTYARD, FLEUR DE LIS SALON, FLEUR DE LIS (A, B, C), DESIRE OYSTER BAR, WOMENS & MENS RR. LEVEL 2: BOARD ROOM, TECHE, BELLE GROVE, OAK ALLEY, MADEWOOD, GRIS GRIS, CHOCTAW, ASSEMBLY AREA, & TERRACE LEVEL 3: POOL BAR REFERENCE SHEET PS-G004.00 FOR A DIAGRAM OF AREA OF RENOVATION SCOPE THAT IS A LEVEL 1 ALTERATION & LEVEL 2 ALTERATION. EMERGENCY PERMIT APPLICATION TO SHORE, REMOVE AND REPLACE WALL THAT IS IN IMMINENT DANGER OF COLLAPSING. According to the attached Engineer’s report dated July 14, 2025, the wall on the Toulouse Street side of the building bearing municipal address 601 Bourbon Street “is well outside of sound engineering and construction tolerances and should be replaced immediately” to prevent a collapse of the wall. This permit request includes the shoring of the Toulouse Street and Bourbon Street (partial) sides of the building; erection of a construction wall around the shoring area; removal of approximately 35 feet of the Toulouse Street wall to the parapet; recapping foundation rebar and concrete; rebuilding the wall three wythe thick; and related cosmetic restoration all in accordance with the attached stamped Engineer’s Masonry Wall Shoring and Repair Plans.
